title: Senior Staff Nurse I/II (NUHS@Home)*(5674)
description:
The NUHS@Home nurse provides nursing care to patients who require acute medical treatment but who are able to be cared for in their homes rather than in a hospital. The nurse works in collaboration with physicians and other healthcare professionals to develop and implement care plans, monitor patient progress, and educate patients and their families on self-care techniques.

The NUHS@Home nurse is competent in performing care assessment and planning, and in the management of patients with identified care needs. S/He provides clinical supervision, precepts the junior nurses and contributes to NUHS@Home nursing training.

Job Responsibilities

Primary Responsibilities and Duties (80%)

  • Conduct patient assessments and develop individualized care plans
  • Administer medications, treatments and procedures as ordered by the physician
  • Monitor and evaluate patient progress and adjust care plans as needed
  • Communicate with physicians, other healthcare professionals, patients and families regarding patient care
  • Educate patients and families on self-care techniques and disease management
  • Ensure patient safety and infection control measures are maintained
  • Document patient care and progress in electronic medical records
  • Participate in quality improvement initiatives
  • Adhere to ethical and legal standards of nursing practice
  • Utilise technology in care delivery

Secondary Responsibilities and Duties (20%)

  • Participates in research, clinical or service quality activities
  • Demonstrates awareness of current nursing and health care practices
  • Assumes additional responsibilities as assigned by Nursing Officers, Senior Nursing Officers, Assistant/Deputy Director (Nursing) and Chief Nurse

Job Specification/Requirements

  • Possess Diploma / Degree in Nursing and must be registered with Singapore Nursing Board
  • Registered Nurse with minimum of 3-5 years of post-registration clinical experience
  • Ability to work independently and as a team
  • Strong organisational, coordination, interpersonal and communication skills
  • Ability to work a flexible schedule, including evenings, weekends and public holidays

postedCompany.description:
The National University Health System (NUHS) plays a key, contributing role in Singapore’s healthcare. Our versatile and experienced team of healthcare professionals has only one aim - to help people get better, and live healthier and longer lives.

We do this by improving medical care, lowering disease recurrence, producing better healthcare professionals, and turning research findings into better medical treatments for diseases that affect Singaporeans. And the reason we’re able to do these things is because we have the best and most dedicated people. Come join us and help shape medicine for our future.
postedCompany.name: NATIONAL UNIVERSITY HEALTH SYSTEM PTE. LTD.
